title: How wide is this box, really?

options: 200px — width sets the full visible size
240px — content 200 + padding 20×2, borders don't take space
mcq.explanation_md: Under the default `content-box`, `width` sets only the **content** area. Padding and border stack on the outside: 200 + 20 + 20 + 5 + 5 = **250px** of visible box. The 270px option is the classic trap — margin *does* push neighbours away, but it's transparent space *around* the box, not part of the box you see, so it doesn't count toward the element's visible width. This surprise is exactly why real stylesheets set `* { box-sizing: border-box; }`, which makes `width: 200px` mean the full 200px including padding and border.
